commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `/status` and `session_status` so session-selected provider/model overrides drive usage, auth, harness, and context lookups, with regression coverage for separate and provider-qualified override shapes.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +158. Total +177 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main passes configured/default provider inputs through the status usage path while the session entry may hold a different selected model, matching the linked live report; I did not run local tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed because the patch has no actionable review finding; normal maintainer review and CI should decide landing.

target.title: [Bug]: /status usage should follow session-selected model after /model switch
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes `/status` so model/auth/usage/context lookup follows the session-selected model after `/model` pins a session to another provider.
- Keeps the configured default visible, so reviewers can still see that new or unpinned sessions use `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`.
- Adds regression coverage for both stored override shapes: separate `providerOverride: "openai"` + `modelOverride: "gpt-5.5"`, and provider-qualified `modelOverride: "openai/gpt-5.5"`.
- Out of scope: changing model selection, fallback execution, provider credentials, config defaults, migrations, or external usage APIs.

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for status model-reference resolver/lookup mapping.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the changed behavior is isolated to status rendering inputs and is covered by focused regressions plus adjacent session-status validation.
- Root cause: The status resolver mapping was split: `buildStatusText` and `buildStatusMessage` rendered session-selected model overrides in some places but, because the selected model resolver was not used for all downstream lookups, usage, auth, harness, and selected-context metadata still came from the configured default provider/model. Provider-qualified `modelOverride` values also stayed unparsed for selected status lookups, so `deepseek` could remain the lookup provider for an `openai/gpt-5.5` session.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch makes the selected model resolver parse provider-qualified selected overrides when no separate provider override exists, then feeds the parsed selected provider/model into the status usage, auth, harness, context, and channel-note lookup paths. This changes the shared resolver boundary used by status output instead of suppressing a DeepSeek balance string after it has already been selected.
- What did NOT change: This only changes `/status` and `session_status` status lookup inputs; it does not change session pinning semantics, configured defaults, runtime fallback behavior, provider credentials, model execution, migrations, or unrelated status sections.

## Linked context

Closes #93322

Related: #93322 reports that `/status` can show `Session selected: openai/gpt-5.5` while still showing DeepSeek usage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `/status` now keeps the configured default line on DeepSeek while resolving the session-selected model, context metadata, and usage/provider decision from OpenAI after a session is pinned to `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw status renderer executed with Node against the patched source, using a real `buildStatusText` call and a session entry matching the reported override shape.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENAI_API_KEY= DEEPSEEK_API_KEY= node --import tsx --input-type=module <status-renderer proof script>`; regression test validation also ran the focused `/status` provider-selection tests and the combined status suites.
- Evidence after fix (copied live output):
  Exit code: 0

  ```text
  🦞 OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (da92615)
  ⏱️ Uptime: gateway 50s · system 9h 37m
  🧠 Configured default: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash
  📌 Session selected: openai/gpt-5.5
  ⚠️ Reason: session override
  ⚠️ This session is pinned to openai/gpt-5.5; config primary deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash will apply to new/unpinned sessions.
  ↩️ Clear with: /model default
  📖 Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/models#selection-source-and-fallback-behavior
  📚 Context: ?/258k · 🧹 Compactions: 0
  🧵 Session: agent:main:main • updated just now
  ⚙️ Execution: direct · Runtime: OpenClaw Default · Think: off · Fast: off · Text: low · elevated
  🪢 Queue: steer (depth 0)

  CHECKS
  selectedOpenAI=true
  configuredDeepSeek=true
  deepSeekBalanceShown=false
  openAIContextShown=true
  ```
- Observed result after fix: the copied runtime output shows `Session selected: openai/gpt-5.5`, keeps `Configured default: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`, resolves OpenAI context metadata as `/258k`, and reports `deepSeekBalanceShown=false`.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps for the status selection path. The proof intentionally avoids a live external OpenAI balance request; provider API availability is outside this status lookup fix.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live output exercises status rendering and provider/model selection locally without contacting external billing or balance endpoints. Regression tests separately assert the provider usage loader receives `openai` rather than `deepseek`.
- Before evidence: the failing regression showed the status text could render `Session selected: openai/gpt-5.5` while still rendering `Usage: Balance ¥42.50` from DeepSeek.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts -t "uses the session-selected model provider for /status usage"`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts -t "uses provider-qualified model overrides for /status usage lookup"`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`

Validation result:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       30 passed (30)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       60 passed (60)
```

Regression coverage added:

- A `/status` regression test covers configured default `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` with session-selected `providerOverride: "openai"` and `modelOverride: "gpt-5.5"`.
- A `/status` regression test covers provider-qualified `modelOverride: "openai/gpt-5.5"` with no separate provider override.
- The tests assert that usage is loaded for OpenAI, DeepSeek balance output is not rendered, OpenAI auth is selected, and selected context metadata follows OpenAI.

What failed before this fix:

- The first regression initially failed because the status text showed the OpenAI session selection but still rendered DeepSeek usage.
- The provider-qualified regression initially failed because the status text displayed `deepseek/openai/gpt-5.5` and used DeepSeek auth/usage/context lookup.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `/status` and `session_status` now report status details for the session-selected/effective provider after `/model` switches the session.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Provider/auth/context selection for status rendering when a session override differs from the configured default.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to status model-reference lookup inputs, keeps configured-default display unchanged, uses the existing selected/active model resolver, and has focused regression coverage plus adjacent `session_status` validation.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local Codex diff review findings were addressed before submission: optional usage mock options, provider-qualified `modelOverride` lookup, and selected context lookup for provider-qualified overrides.
